name: Stir Fried Velvet Spinach with Glass Noodles
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large covered saucepan, bring 2 to 2-1/2 quarts of unsalted water to a boil over high heat.
2. Discard any straggly spinach leaves and white roots, then use scissors to cut stems and large leaves into 2-inch sections.
3. Cut bulky stem clusters and any red root tips intact.
4. In a large bowl, plunge the cut spinach into several changes of cold water, gently pumping up and down to remove grit.
5. Drain in colander and shake off excess water.
6. Plunge spinach into boiling water for 1 minute, then drain in colander and flush with cold running water until chilled.
7. Press lightly with palms of hands to remove excess water and fluff the spinach gently to loosen it.
8. Cover bean threads with 3 to 4 cups of heated water (use hot water if from Taiwan or Thailand, boiling water if from the People's Republic of China) without removing the binding of rubber bands or strings.
9. When pliable after about 10 seconds, use scissors to cut through loop ends or center of skein into manageable 4- to 5-inch lengths.
10. Cut and discard the rubber bands or strings, then swirl bean threads in water with a spatula to disperse them.
11. Rinse briefly with cool water and drain again when bean threads are firm to the touch.
12. Heat a skillet over high heat until hot enough to evaporate a bead of water on contact, then add oil and swirl to coat pan.
13. Reduce heat to medium-high; when hot enough to sizzle a bit of spinach on contact, add spinach and toss briskly for another minute.
14. Add noodles and blend with several quick stirs until heated through.
15. Add sesame oil, toss briskly to combine, then remove from heat.
16. Arrange rosy-tipped spinach stems on top if desired and serve at once.
